title: Interplay of Valley, Orbital, Spin, and Layer Degrees of Freedom in Ta$_2$CS$_2$ MXene
authors: Kunal Dutta, Anupam Mondal, Sayantika Bhowal, Subhradip Ghosh, Indra Dasgupta
abstract: We show that the MXene Ta$_2$CS$_2$ provides an excellent platform for hosting multiple coupled degrees of freedom, viz., valley, spin, orbital, and layer. The interplay among these degrees of freedom gives rise to a range of intriguing properties in reciprocal space, including valley-orbital and orbital-layer coupling. In the presence of spin-orbit interaction, these couplings lead to valley-dependent and layer-dependent spin splitting of the electronic bands. We further show that the intrinsic electric polarization in Ta$_2$CS$_2$ introduces an additional tuning parameter, enabling control over these coupled degrees of freedom and resulting in switchable valley-dependent orbital moments and Zeeman-like spin splitting. We demonstrate that these nontrivial orbital and spin textures manifest in the orbital and spin Hall effects, respectively. Our results establish noncentrosymmetric MXenes as a promising platform for exploring the interplay among multiple degrees of freedom, their tunability, and the resulting orbital and spin transport phenomena in these two-dimensional materials, thereby paving the way for next-generation spin-orbitronic devices.
summary: This paper explores how the coupling of multiple degrees of freedom in the MXene Ta2CS2 enables controllable electronic properties. By using intrinsic electric polarization, one can switch orbital moments and spin splitting, suggesting potential applications in next-generation spin-orbitronic devices.
main_problem: Investigating the interplay between valley, orbital, spin, and layer degrees of freedom in the MXene Ta2CS2 and how these couplings affect electronic band structures and transport.
main_result: The study identifies valley-orbital and orbital-layer couplings that lead to valley- and layer-dependent spin splitting, which can be tuned via intrinsic electric polarization to produce switchable orbital moments and Zeeman-like spin splitting.
method: Theoretical analysis of electronic band structures and transport phenomena (orbital and spin Hall effects) in the presence of spin-orbit interaction and electric polarization.
model_or_system: The 2D MXene Ta2CS2, a noncentrosymmetric material characterized by coupled valley, spin, orbital, and layer degrees of freedom.
key_observables: Valley-dependent spin splitting, orbital and spin Hall effects, and switchable valley-dependent orbital moments.
important_parameters: Spin-orbit interaction strength and intrinsic electric polarization.
paper_structure: The paper discusses the coupling of degrees of freedom in Ta2CS2, the effect of spin-orbit interaction on band splitting, the role of electric polarization in tuning these properties, and the resulting manifestation in Hall effects.
